questions.text: What does the term 'congruent triangles' mean?

options.b: Triangles that are exactly identical in shape and size

questions.explanation: Congruent triangles are triangles that are identical in both shape and size, meaning all corresponding sides and angles are equal.

questions.text: Which of the following is NOT a criterion for triangle congruence?
questions.explanation: AAA (Angle-Angle-Angle) only proves similarity, not congruence, because it does not guarantee equal side lengths.

questions.text: In the SSS congruence criterion, what must be equal?

options.b: Two pairs of sides and the included angle
options.d: Two pairs of angles and one side

questions.explanation: SSS criterion requires all three pairs of corresponding sides of two triangles to be equal for congruence.

questions.text: Which congruence criterion uses two sides and the included angle?
questions.explanation: SAS (Side-Angle-Side) criterion states that if two sides and the included angle of one triangle are equal to those of another, the triangles are congruent.

questions.text: What is the RHS congruence criterion used for?
questions.explanation: RHS (Right angle-Hypotenuse-Side) criterion applies only to right-angled triangles.

questions.text: If two triangles have two angles and one side equal, which congruence criterion applies?
questions.explanation: ASA (Angle-Side-Angle) or AAS (Angle-Angle-Side) criterion applies when two angles and one side are equal.

questions.text: What does CPCT stand for in congruent triangles?
questions.explanation: CPCT means Corresponding Parts of Congruent Triangles, which are equal once triangles are proven congruent.

questions.text: If ∆ABC ≅ ∆DEF, then which of the following is true?

options.a: AB = DE, BC = EF, AC = DF
options.b: AB = EF, BC = DE, AC = DF
options.c: AB = DF, BC = DE, AC = EF
options.d: AB = DE, BC = DF, AC = EF

questions.explanation: Corresponding sides of congruent triangles are equal in the same order: AB = DE, BC = EF, AC = DF.

questions.text: Which of the following pairs of sides and angle can prove congruence by SAS?

options.a: Side AB, side BC, angle A
options.b: Side AB, angle B, side BC
options.c: Side AB, angle B, side AC
options.d: Side AB, side AC, angle B

questions.explanation: SAS requires two sides and the included angle (the angle between the two sides). Here, side AB and AC with included angle B satisfy SAS.

questions.text: In which case can two triangles be congruent by ASA?

options.a: Two angles and the side opposite one angle are equal
options.b: Two sides and the included angle are equal
options.c: Two sides and the angle opposite one side are equal

questions.explanation: ASA requires two angles and the included side (side between the two angles) to be equal; AAS also works if two angles and any one side are equal.

questions.text: If two triangles are congruent, what can be said about their corresponding angles?
questions.explanation: Corresponding angles of congruent triangles are equal.

questions.text: Which of the following is a valid application of congruent triangles?

options.b: Proving two line segments are equal
options.c: Finding the volume of a cube

questions.explanation: Congruent triangles help prove equality of corresponding parts such as line segments.

questions.text: If two right-angled triangles have equal hypotenuses and one equal leg, then they are congruent by which criterion?
questions.explanation: RHS criterion states that right-angled triangles with equal hypotenuse and one leg are congruent.

questions.text: Which of the following is TRUE for congruent triangles?

options.c: Both corresponding sides and angles are equal

questions.explanation: Congruent triangles have all corresponding sides and angles equal.

questions.text: In proving two triangles congruent, which of the following steps is NOT necessary?

options.b: Check equality of parts according to a criterion

questions.explanation: Calculating area is not necessary for proving congruence; congruence depends on sides and angles, not area.

questions.text: Which congruence criterion can be used if two triangles have two pairs of equal angles and one pair of equal sides not included between the angles?
questions.explanation: AAS (Angle-Angle-Side) applies when two angles and any one side (not necessarily included) are equal.

questions.text: Two triangles have sides 5 cm, 7 cm, and 9 cm respectively. If the corresponding sides of the second triangle are 7 cm, 9 cm, and 5 cm, are the triangles congruent?

options.b: No, because the order of sides matters
options.d: No, because sides are different lengths

questions.explanation: For SSS, corresponding sides must be in the same order; rearranging side lengths changes the triangle's shape, so they are not congruent.

questions.text: If two triangles are congruent, which of the following statements is correct?
questions.explanation: CPCT implies all corresponding parts, including medians and altitudes, are equal in congruent triangles.

questions.text: In a proof, if you know two sides and the angle opposite one side are equal in two triangles, which congruence criterion can be applied?
questions.explanation: Two angles and one side (AAS) congruence applies if the side is opposite one of the equal angles.

questions.text: Which of these statements is FALSE about congruent triangles?

options.d: They have equal corresponding altitudes only if they are isosceles

questions.explanation: Corresponding altitudes of congruent triangles are always equal, regardless of triangle type.

questions.text: In a triangular plot of land, two sides measure 50 m and 70 m, and the angle between them is 60°. A similar triangular plot has two sides measuring 50 m and 70 m with the angle between them also 60°. Which congruence criterion proves these two plots are congruent?
questions.explanation: Two sides and the included angle are equal in both triangles, so the SAS (Side-Angle-Side) criterion applies.

questions.text: Two triangular windows in a house have sides measuring 3 m, 4 m, and 5 m each. If one triangle is rotated, which criterion can be used to prove that the two windows are congruent?
questions.explanation: All three corresponding sides are equal, so the SSS (Side-Side-Side) criterion proves congruence.

questions.text: A carpenter is making two triangular wooden frames. Both have one side measuring 8 cm and two adjacent angles measuring 45° and 60°. Which congruence criterion can the carpenter use to ensure the frames are congruent?
questions.explanation: One side and the two adjacent angles are equal, so ASA (Angle-Side-Angle) criterion applies.

questions.text: Two right-angled triangular tiles have hypotenuses measuring 13 cm and one leg measuring 5 cm each. Which criterion will prove that the two tiles are congruent?
questions.explanation: In right-angled triangles, if the hypotenuse and one leg are equal, the RHS (Right angle-Hypotenuse-Side) criterion applies.

questions.text: In two triangular fields, one has sides 100 m, 120 m and an included angle of 90°, and the second has sides 120 m, 100 m and an included angle of 90°. Are these fields congruent?

options.b: No, because the order of sides is different

questions.explanation: The order of sides does not matter; two sides and the included angle are equal, so SAS applies.

questions.text: A triangular banner has two angles measuring 40° and 70°, and the side opposite the 40° angle is 10 m. Another banner has the same two angles and a side of 10 m opposite the 40° angle. Which congruence criterion proves they are congruent?
questions.explanation: Two angles and a non-included side are equal, so AAS (Angle-Angle-Side) criterion applies.

questions.text: Two triangular road signs have sides measuring 6 m, 8 m, and 10 m. If one sign is flipped, which congruence criterion confirms the signs are congruent?
questions.explanation: All three sides are equal, so SSS criterion proves congruence regardless of orientation.

questions.text: Two triangular garden beds have one side of 15 m and two angles adjacent to that side measuring 50° and 60°. Which criterion can be used to prove the beds are congruent?
questions.explanation: One side and the two adjacent angles are equal, so ASA criterion applies.

questions.text: In two triangular billboards, the hypotenuse is 25 m and one leg is 7 m. Which congruence criterion applies to prove the billboards are congruent?
questions.explanation: Right-angled triangles with equal hypotenuse and one leg are congruent by RHS criterion.

questions.text: Two triangular flags have sides 9 m, 12 m and an included angle of 45°. A third flag has sides 12 m, 9 m and the included angle of 45°. Are the first two flags congruent to the third?
questions.explanation: Order of sides does not affect congruence; two sides and included angle equal means SAS applies.

questions.text: In triangle ABC, AB = AC and angle BAC = 40°. Point D lies on BC such that AD bisects angle BAC. If triangle ABD is congruent to triangle ACD, find the measure of angle ABC.
questions.explanation: Since AB = AC, triangle ABC is isosceles with base BC. Angle BAC = 40°, so angles ABC and ACB are equal. Sum of angles in triangle ABC is 180°, so 40° + 2x = 180°, giving x = 70°. Since AD bisects angle BAC and triangles ABD and ACD are congruent by SAS, angle ABC = 70°.

questions.text: In triangle PQR, point S lies on side PR such that QS is perpendicular to PR. Given that triangle PQS is congruent to triangle QRS, prove that triangle PQR is isosceles and find which sides are equal.
questions.explanation: Triangles PQS and QRS are congruent by RHS (right angle, hypotenuse, side). Since QS is common and perpendicular to PR, the congruence implies PQ = QR. Hence, triangle PQR is isosceles with PQ = QR.

questions.text: In triangle XYZ, XY = XZ and angle YXZ = 50°. Point W lies on YZ such that XW bisects angle YXZ. Using congruence criteria, prove that triangles XYW and XZW are congruent and find angle YWZ.
questions.explanation: Since XY = XZ and XW bisects angle YXZ, triangles XYW and XZW are congruent by SAS (side-angle-side). Angles at Y and Z are equal; each is (180° - 50°)/2 = 65°. Angle YWZ is external to triangle XYW and equals 180° - 65° - 35° = 80°.

questions.text: Triangle DEF is right-angled at E. If DE = EF and triangle DEF is congruent to triangle DGF where G lies on DF, prove that triangle EGF is right-angled and find the measure of angle EGF.
questions.explanation: Given DE = EF and triangle DEF is right angled at E, triangle DEF is isosceles right angled. Since triangle DEF ≅ triangle DGF, triangle EGF shares properties making it right angled at G with angle 90°.

questions.text: In triangle ABC, AB = AC and point D is midpoint of BC. If triangle ABD is congruent to triangle ACD, prove that AD is perpendicular to BC and find angle ADB.
questions.explanation: Since AB = AC and D is midpoint of BC, triangles ABD and ACD are congruent by SSS. Hence, AD bisects BC and is perpendicular to it. Therefore, angle ADB = 90°.

questions.text: In triangle MNO, MN = MO and point P lies on NO such that MP bisects angle NMO. If triangle MNP is congruent to triangle MOP, find the measure of angle NMO and prove the congruence using appropriate criteria.

options.a: Angle NMO = 40°, triangles congruent by SAS
options.b: Angle NMO = 50°, triangles congruent by ASA
options.c: Angle NMO = 60°, triangles congruent by RHS
options.d: Angle NMO = 70°, triangles congruent by SSS

questions.explanation: Since MN = MO and MP bisects angle NMO, triangles MNP and MOP are congruent by SAS (two sides and included angle). Given this, angle NMO = 40° satisfies the conditions.

questions.text: In triangle ABC, AB = AC and angle B = 40°. Find angle C using the congruence criteria and justify your answer.
questions.explanation: Since AB = AC, triangle ABC is isosceles and angles opposite equal sides are equal. Therefore, angle C = angle B = 40°.

questions.text: Two triangles PQR and XYZ are such that PQ = XY, QR = YZ and angle Q = angle Y. State the congruence criterion used to prove △PQR ≅ △XYZ and write the corresponding parts that are congruent.
questions.correct_answer: SAS; PR = XZ, angle P = angle X, angle R = angle Z
accepted_forms: SAS; PR = XZ, angle P = angle X, angle R = angle Z
SAS and corresponding sides and angles
SAS criterion, corresponding parts are PR = XZ, angle P = angle X, angle R = angle Z
questions.explanation: Given two sides and the included angle are equal (PQ=XY, QR=YZ, angle Q=angle Y), the SAS criterion applies. Hence, the triangles are congruent and corresponding parts PR=XZ, angle P=angle X, angle R=angle Z.

questions.text: In right-angled triangles ABC and DEF, right angled at B and E respectively, if AC = DF and AB = DE, prove that △ABC ≅ △DEF by stating the congruence criterion.
accepted_forms: RHS criterion and triangles are congruent
questions.explanation: Both triangles are right angled at B and E. Given hypotenuse AC = DF and side AB = DE, by RHS criterion, △ABC ≅ △DEF.

questions.text: Triangle PQR has sides PQ = 5 cm, QR = 7 cm and PR = 6 cm. Triangle XYZ has sides XY = 7 cm, YZ = 6 cm and XZ = 5 cm. Are the two triangles congruent? Justify your answer.
accepted_forms: Yes, triangles are congruent using SSS
questions.explanation: Rearranging sides of XYZ as 5 cm, 6 cm, 7 cm matches sides of PQR. Since all three sides are equal, triangles are congruent by SSS criterion.

questions.text: In △ABC, angle A = 60°, angle B = 50°, and side AC = 8 cm. In △PQR, angle P = 60°, angle Q = 50°, and side PR = 8 cm. Are the two triangles congruent? Name the criterion used.
questions.explanation: Two angles and the included side are equal (angle A=angle P=60°, angle B=angle Q=50°, side AC=PR=8 cm), so triangles are congruent by ASA.

questions.text: In △ABC and △DEF, AB = DE, BC = EF, and angle B = angle E. If the triangles are congruent, find the length of AC given DF = 10 cm.
questions.explanation: By SAS criterion (two sides and included angle equal), triangles are congruent, so corresponding side AC = DF = 10 cm.

questions.text: Prove that the base angles of an isosceles triangle are equal using the congruence of triangles.
questions.correct_answer: By drawing the altitude from the vertex to base, two right triangles are formed which are congruent by RHS criterion; hence base angles are equal.
accepted_forms: By drawing altitude, two right triangles are congruent by RHS; base angles equal
Altitude creates two congruent triangles by RHS, so base angles equal
Using RHS congruence of two right triangles formed by altitude, base angles are equal
questions.explanation: Drawing altitude divides isosceles triangle into two right triangles with equal hypotenuse and side; by RHS criterion, triangles are congruent, so base angles are equal.

questions.text: In △ABC and △DEF, angle A = angle D, side AB = side DE, and angle B = angle E. Are the triangles congruent? If yes, state the criterion used.
accepted_forms: Yes, by ASA or AAS criterion
questions.explanation: Two angles and the included side are equal, so triangles are congruent by ASA criterion (or AAS if side is not included).

questions.text: In △PQR, side PQ = 7 cm, side PR = 5 cm, and angle QPR = 60°. In △XYZ, side XY = 7 cm, side XZ = 5 cm, and angle YXZ = 60°. Are the triangles congruent? Name the criterion.
questions.explanation: Two sides and included angle are equal in both triangles, so they are congruent by SAS criterion.

questions.text: Using CPCT, if two triangles are congruent, what can be said about their corresponding altitudes? Justify your answer.
questions.correct_answer: Corresponding altitudes are equal because all corresponding parts of congruent triangles, including altitudes, are equal.
accepted_forms: Corresponding altitudes are equal as CPCT states all corresponding parts are equal
Altitudes corresponding to congruent triangles are equal by CPCT
CPCT implies corresponding altitudes are equal
questions.explanation: CPCT means Corresponding Parts of Congruent Triangles are equal, so corresponding altitudes must be equal.

questions.case_text: In a village fair, two triangular flags are designed to be exactly the same size and shape for decoration. Each flag has sides measuring 3 m, 4 m, and 5 m. The organizer wants to confirm if the two flags are congruent using the SSS criterion before hanging them on the main gate.

subparts.text: Which congruence criterion should the organizer use to confirm the flags are congruent?
subparts.explanation: Since all three sides of both triangular flags are equal (3 m, 4 m, and 5 m), the SSS criterion confirms congruence.

subparts.text: If one flag is rotated and placed over the other, which corresponding parts will exactly match?

options.a: Only the sides of length 3 m

subparts.explanation: Congruent triangles have all corresponding sides and angles equal, so all parts will match exactly.

subparts.text: What is the length of the longest side of each flag?
subparts.explanation: The longest side among 3 m, 4 m, and 5 m is 5 m.

questions.case_text: A civil engineer is designing two triangular park benches with right-angled triangular supports. Each support has one right angle, a hypotenuse of 10 ft, and one side measuring 6 ft. The engineer wants to prove that the two supports are congruent using the RHS criterion before mass production.

subparts.text: Which parts of the triangles does the RHS criterion compare to prove congruence?

options.a: Two sides and the included angle
options.b: Hypotenuse and one side with the right angle

subparts.explanation: RHS criterion states that two right-angled triangles are congruent if their hypotenuse and one side are equal.

subparts.text: If the other side of the support is calculated using Pythagoras theorem, what is its length?
subparts.explanation: Using Pythagoras theorem: other side = √(10² - 6²) = √(100 - 36) = √64 = 8 ft.

subparts.text: State the congruence criterion used to prove the two supports are congruent.
subparts.explanation: The RHS criterion is used for congruence of right-angled triangles based on hypotenuse and one side.
